agent2 (Round 2 summarized): The child likely has post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis due to an immune complex-mediated type III hypersensitivity response triggered by prior group A Streptococcus infection. This explains her recent hematuria, significant proteinuria, peripheral edema, and historical sore throat consistent with acute pharyngitis after exposure to Group A Strep.

\boxed{C}

We are asked only for a two-sentence summary focusing solely on *key clinical points* related to the question.

Final Summary:  
This case is most consistent with post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis secondary to a previous group A strep pharyngitis; antigen-antibody deposits trigger inflammatory damage leading directly to hematuria and proteinuria. Edema reflects volume overload associated with nephrotic-range protein loss as seen here clinically.\boxed{C}
